Title: Repository documentation structure
Objective: Create the agreed repository documentation layout and replace codex.md with AGENTS.md.
In Scope: README.md; AGENTS.md; docs directory; internal links.
Acceptance Criteria: All agreed Markdown files exist; links resolve; obsolete codex.md is absent.
Expected Tests: Link/path validation script or manual path check.
Manual Verification: Open each Markdown file and follow its main links.

Title: Requirements specification
Objective: Record the agreed functional and non-functional requirements as the source of truth.
In Scope: Workflow; job fields; assessment; CV generation; French path; upload path; tracking; exclusions.
Acceptance Criteria: Requirements cover every agreed workflow step and explicitly exclude automatic submission.
Manual Verification: Compare the document with the approved conversation decisions.

Title: Screen specification
Objective: Document the Jobs dashboard, Job Details tabs, Background Runs and Settings screens.
In Scope: Fields; filters; table actions; direct DOCX opening; approval actions.
Out of Scope: Visual design system or advanced mock-ups.
Acceptance Criteria: Every user action is assigned to a screen and no required action is missing.
Manual Verification: Walk through the four principal user journeys.

Title: Architecture and ADRs
Objective: Record the chosen stack and the main technical decisions.
In Scope: Streamlit; SQLite; OpenAI; DOCX; sequential default; hybrid document handling; local Word editing.
Acceptance Criteria: Architecture and decision records are consistent and reference the requirements.
Manual Verification: Check each decision against the agreed constraints.

Title: GitHub-led backlog and ticket workflow
Objective: Establish GitHub Issues as the operational source of truth while retaining a tracked CSV overview and concise repository roadmap.
In Scope: GitHub issues and milestones; ticket IDs; dependencies; acceptance criteria; implementation-backlog.csv; PowerShell importer; roadmap; Codex workflow.
Out of Scope: Detailed ticket specifications duplicated in Markdown; Excel tracker; implementing backlog tickets.
Acceptance Criteria: Every CSV ticket has one canonical GitHub issue; CSV and GitHub IDs match; the roadmap identifies GitHub as source of truth; the importer supports dry runs repeat execution and suffixed IDs; the Excel tracker is absent.
Expected Tests: Offline importer tests; CSV duplicate-ID check; CSV-to-GitHub ID comparison; documentation link check.
Manual Verification: Open the roadmap and GitHub links; filter issues by milestone priority size and status.

Title: Python project scaffold
Objective: Create a minimal installable Python project with a working Streamlit entry point.
In Scope: pyproject.toml; src package; tests; pytest; Ruff; basic app entry.
Acceptance Criteria: Project installs; Streamlit starts; empty test suite and Ruff checks run successfully.
Expected Tests: Smoke test for imports and app entry.
Manual Verification: Run the documented start command.

Title: PowerShell developer task runner
Objective: Provide one PowerShell entry point for common local development commands.
In Scope: Root-level dev.ps1; env target; activate target; test target; lint target; ui target; help output; prerequisite errors; README and AGENTS commands.
Out of Scope: Starting the background worker; starting UI and worker together; GNU Make support; Linux/macOS support; packaging or installation.
Acceptance Criteria: The task runner creates and activates .venv; runs pytest and Ruff; starts Streamlit; displays help; fails clearly when prerequisites are missing; supported commands are documented.
Manual Verification: Run each target on Windows and confirm help and prerequisite errors.
Notes: Keep worker and combined launcher work under later tickets.

Title: Typed application configuration
Objective: Implement environment-based settings with safe defaults.
In Scope: Database path; CV folder; OpenAI key; worker counts; defaults for source/location/language.
Acceptance Criteria: Configuration loads from environment; defaults are documented; invalid values fail clearly.
Expected Tests: Unit tests for defaults, overrides and invalid worker counts.
Manual Verification: Start with and without a local .env file.

Title: Local directory management
Objective: Create and validate the required private local directories.
In Scope: database; reference assets; generated CVs; uploaded CVs; logs; gitignore rules.
Acceptance Criteria: Directories are created idempotently and private paths are ignored by Git.
Expected Tests: Unit test for idempotent directory creation.
Manual Verification: Delete a test directory and restart the app.

Title: SQLite connection and migrations
Objective: Provide database sessions and a controlled migration mechanism.
In Scope: SQLAlchemy/SQLModel engine; sessions; migration bootstrap; health check.
Acceptance Criteria: A new database can be created; a health check succeeds; migrations are repeatable.
Expected Tests: Integration tests against a temporary SQLite database.
Manual Verification: Start twice and confirm no duplicate schema errors.

Title: Streamlit navigation shell
Objective: Create the application navigation and empty page shells.
In Scope: Jobs; Background Runs; Settings; consistent page title and error boundary.
Acceptance Criteria: All three pages are reachable and the app starts without data.
Manual Verification: Navigate through every page.

Title: Job database model
Objective: Create the jobs table with the agreed fields and constraints.
In Scope: Company; title; UK/FR/CH; EN/FR; source; URL; JD; editable date; notes; decision; application fields.
Acceptance Criteria: Migration creates the table; enums/constraints are enforced; timestamps update correctly.
Manual Verification: Create a job directly through a test script.

Title: Job repository service
Objective: Implement CRUD operations without coupling them to Streamlit.
In Scope: Create; read; update; list; delete; basic query filters.
Acceptance Criteria: CRUD operations work and transactions roll back on failure.
Manual Verification: Create, edit and delete a sample job.

Title: Add-job form
Objective: Create the job-entry form with agreed defaults and validation.
In Scope: LinkedIn default; current date default but editable; required company/title/JD; location/language choices.
Out of Scope: URL parsing or automated JD extraction.
Acceptance Criteria: A valid job saves; missing required fields show useful messages; defaults are correct.
Manual Verification: Add jobs from LinkedIn and company-site sources.

Title: Edit-job form
Objective: Allow jobs to be edited without automatically launching assessment.
In Scope: All job fields; editable date added; save/cancel actions.
Acceptance Criteria: Edits persist and no assessment task is created.
Manual Verification: Edit the JD and confirm no background task starts.

Title: Initial Jobs dashboard table
Objective: Display stored jobs in the primary application table.
In Scope: Core columns; row selection; sorting; open Job Details.
Out of Scope: Assessment, CV and KPI columns that depend on later tables.
Acceptance Criteria: Rows reflect the database and selected rows can be identified reliably.
Manual Verification: Add several jobs and sort/select them.

Title: Jobs dashboard filters
Objective: Add the agreed filters without changing underlying records.
In Scope: Text search; location; language; source; user decision; free-text application status.
Acceptance Criteria: Filters can be combined and cleared; results are correct.
Manual Verification: Test combinations across at least five sample jobs.

Title: Reference-asset database model
Objective: Store metadata for documents, prompts, templates and French examples.
In Scope: Asset type; version; local path; active flag; hash; processing status; OpenAI IDs; dates.
Acceptance Criteria: One active asset per single-instance type is enforceable; multiple French examples are supported.
Manual Verification: Insert current and inactive versions.

Title: DOCX validation and local storage
Objective: Validate and store uploaded reference DOCX files safely.
In Scope: Extension and file checks; immutable versioned filename; hashing; duplicate detection.
Acceptance Criteria: Valid DOCX files are stored; invalid/corrupt files are rejected; duplicates are detected.
Expected Tests: Unit tests with valid, invalid and duplicate fixtures.
Manual Verification: Upload each supported asset type.

Title: Prompt storage and editing
Objective: Store the assessment prompt, four English CV prompts and two additional French prompts.
In Scope: Text editing; ordered pipeline position; versions; activation.
Acceptance Criteria: Prompts can be created, edited and activated; French prompts are ordered after English prompts.
Manual Verification: Edit a prompt and confirm a new version becomes active.

Title: Settings asset overview
Objective: Show the currently active reference inputs and their status.
In Scope: Filename/name; version; upload date; processing status; replace/edit actions.
Acceptance Criteria: All required asset categories are visible and missing required assets are obvious.
Manual Verification: Open Settings on an empty and populated database.

Title: Replace active local asset
Objective: Allow a new local asset version without deleting prior local metadata.
In Scope: Upload; version assignment; active/inactive switch after local validation.
Acceptance Criteria: The prior asset remains available as inactive metadata and the new local file is active only after validation.
Expected Tests: Integration tests for replacement and rollback on failure.
Manual Verification: Replace Document A with a test copy.

Title: OpenAI file upload service
Objective: Upload authorised DOCX files to OpenAI and persist returned identifiers.
In Scope: Client wrapper; retries; errors; user_data purpose or current recommended equivalent.
Acceptance Criteria: A DOCX can be uploaded and its file ID is stored; failures are recoverable.
Expected Tests: Mocked unit tests plus an opt-in integration test.
Manual Verification: Upload a small test DOCX using a development API key.

Title: Document B vector-store lifecycle
Objective: Create and activate a vector store for each new Document B version.
In Scope: Attach file; poll indexing; validation search; activation; prior store inactive.
Acceptance Criteria: A new store is activated only when the file status is completed and validation succeeds.
Expected Tests: Mocked lifecycle tests; optional OpenAI integration test.
Manual Verification: Upload a new B version and confirm old metadata remains inactive.

Title: Inactive vector-store cleanup
Objective: Provide an explicit cleanup action for inactive OpenAI stores and files.
In Scope: List inactive stores; confirmation; delete association/store; retain local DOCX and metadata.
Acceptance Criteria: The user can delete an inactive remote store without affecting the active version.
Manual Verification: Delete a test inactive store and confirm the active store remains usable.

Title: Document B heading extraction
Objective: Extract the Word heading hierarchy and section content for deterministic routing.
In Scope: Heading styles; numbered-heading fallback; section IDs; ordered text; version reference.
Acceptance Criteria: Known sections can be retrieved by stable IDs and ordering matches the DOCX.
Expected Tests: Unit tests with representative heading/table fixtures.
Manual Verification: Inspect extracted sections for several B headings.

Title: Full Document A input preparation
Objective: Prepare the active Document A as complete authorised context for assessment calls.
In Scope: Active-file lookup; upload/file reference; local fallback text extraction; version metadata.
Out of Scope: Splitting or semantic retrieval of Document A.
Acceptance Criteria: The context builder always references the complete active Document A and records its version.
Expected Tests: Unit tests for missing/inactive asset handling.
Manual Verification: Preview the assembled A context metadata.

Title: Background-task database model
Objective: Persist queued work and its lifecycle.
In Scope: Job; operation; status; batch ID; timestamps; retry count; error; payload metadata.
Acceptance Criteria: Tasks and batches can be created and queried; status transitions are constrained.
Manual Verification: Create a batch with several dummy tasks.

Title: Sequential worker process
Objective: Process queued tasks one at a time by default outside Streamlit request execution.
In Scope: Polling loop; handler registry; clean shutdown; one worker.
Acceptance Criteria: Dummy tasks move from pending to completed sequentially and Streamlit remains responsive.
Expected Tests: Worker integration tests with dummy handlers.
Manual Verification: Queue tasks with delays and observe order.

Title: Worker recovery and interruption
Objective: Make abandoned running tasks recoverable after a crash or restart.
In Scope: Startup scan; INTERRUPTED state; retry action; idempotency guard.
Out of Scope: Automatic resume inside a partially completed model call.
Acceptance Criteria: A simulated crash leaves no permanently running task and interrupted work can be retried.
Manual Verification: Terminate the worker during a dummy task and restart.

Title: Background Runs screen
Objective: Display task and batch progress with useful errors and retry actions.
In Scope: Operation; job; batch; status; start/end; error; retry; filters.
Out of Scope: Cancellation of an already running OpenAI call.
Acceptance Criteria: The screen updates and a failed task can be retried without affecting completed tasks.
Manual Verification: Run a mixed successful/failed dummy batch.

Title: Configurable concurrency
Objective: Permit controlled parallel task execution while keeping one worker as the default.
In Scope: Assessment and CV worker-count settings; upper-bound validation; separate DB sessions.
Acceptance Criteria: Worker count 1 is sequential; a higher valid value processes tasks concurrently without DB errors.
Expected Tests: Concurrency integration tests with dummy handlers.
Manual Verification: Compare execution time with worker count 1 and 2.

Title: LLM-call usage table
Objective: Persist token usage, duration and response metadata for every OpenAI call.
In Scope: Operation; pipeline step; model; input/cached/output/total tokens; duration; status; response ID.
Acceptance Criteria: Successful and failed calls can be recorded and aggregated by job and operation.
Manual Verification: Insert sample calls and inspect totals.

Title: Assessment database model
Objective: Store one current structured assessment per job.
In Scope: Visible fields; internal Document B handover; versions; stale source timestamp; error.
Acceptance Criteria: Migration creates the model and completed/failed results can be persisted.
Manual Verification: Insert a complete sample assessment.

Title: Assessment structured-output schema
Objective: Define and validate the exact model response contract.
In Scope: Summary; role family; decision; fit; interview range; signals; risks; lane; evidence; constraints.
Acceptance Criteria: Valid examples parse and invalid ranges/enums fail with clear errors.
Manual Verification: Validate a saved example from a prior assessment.

Title: Assessment context builder
Objective: Assemble the authorised assessment request from the complete Document A and job information.
In Scope: Assessment prompt; full A; company/title/location/language; full JD; schema; version metadata.
Out of Scope: Document B content or CV wording.
Acceptance Criteria: The request contains the full active A and no B content; missing assets stop the task clearly.
Manual Verification: Inspect a rendered request without sending it.

Title: Single OpenAI assessment call
Objective: Run and validate one assessment through the OpenAI Responses API.
In Scope: Client call; structured output; bounded retry; token/time capture.
Acceptance Criteria: One representative JD returns a valid assessment or a useful failure record.
Expected Tests: Mocked client tests and opt-in integration test.
Manual Verification: Assess one historical job and inspect usage.

Title: Assessment persistence service
Objective: Save successful results and failures atomically against the job.
In Scope: Current-result replacement; versions; timestamps; failure details.
Acceptance Criteria: A completed result replaces the current result only after validation; failure does not erase a prior valid result.
Expected Tests: Integration tests for success, failure and reassessment.
Manual Verification: Reassess a sample job with a forced failure.

Title: Assessment worker handler
Objective: Connect queued assessment tasks to the assessment service.
In Scope: Task payload; status updates; independent failure; usage records.
Acceptance Criteria: Queued assessment tasks complete independently and update task/job state correctly.
Manual Verification: Queue two assessments with one deliberate failure.

Title: Assess selected jobs action
Objective: Queue assessments for multiple selected dashboard rows in one click.
In Scope: Eligibility checks; confirmation; one task per job; batch ID.
Acceptance Criteria: Selected eligible jobs are queued once and unselected jobs are untouched.
Manual Verification: Select three rows and start one batch.

Title: Reassess selected jobs action
Objective: Allow completed or failed jobs to be reassessed in one batch.
In Scope: Manual selection; current valid result retained until replacement succeeds.
Acceptance Criteria: Multiple assessed jobs can be relaunched and failures preserve prior valid results.
Expected Tests: Integration tests for mixed reassessment outcomes.
Manual Verification: Reassess several historical jobs.

Title: Stale-assessment detection
Objective: Flag an assessment when assessment-relevant job fields change.
In Scope: Company; title; location; language; JD; source timestamp comparison.
Out of Scope: Automatic reassessment or staleness for URL/source/notes only.
Acceptance Criteria: Relevant edits set stale; non-relevant edits do not; the existing assessment remains accessible.
Manual Verification: Edit notes, then edit the JD and compare indicators.

Title: Assessment detail tab
Objective: Display the complete assessment and internal CV handover data.
In Scope: All visible fields; evidence anchors; gaps; recommended lane; versions; stale warning.
Acceptance Criteria: The tab shows current data and clearly distinguishes model output from user choices.
Manual Verification: Open assessed, failed and stale jobs.

Title: User application decision
Objective: Record the user's decision independently of the model recommendation.
In Scope: Undecided; Pursue; Do not pursue; notes.
Acceptance Criteria: The user decision persists and the model decision remains unchanged and visible.
Manual Verification: Set and change each decision value.

Title: CV lane selection
Objective: Allow the user to confirm or change the recommended Document B lane.
In Scope: Recommended lane display; selected lane; optional secondary angle.
Acceptance Criteria: The selected lane defaults to the recommendation and can be changed before generation.
Manual Verification: Choose a different lane and reopen the job.

Title: Select for CV generation
Objective: Mark one or more pursued jobs as ready for CV generation.
In Scope: Dashboard batch action; explicit status; eligibility and missing-assessment warnings.
Acceptance Criteria: Eligible jobs receive the selected status; ineligible jobs return clear messages.
Manual Verification: Select a mix of eligible and ineligible rows.

Title: Assessment columns on Jobs dashboard
Objective: Add assessment state and decision information to the primary table.
In Scope: Assessment status; decision; fit score; interview probability; stale indicator.
Acceptance Criteria: Columns show current values and remain sortable/filterable.
Manual Verification: Compare table values with Job Details.

Title: CV and application columns on Jobs dashboard
Objective: Reserve and populate workflow columns needed for later stages.
In Scope: CV status; source; direct open action placeholder; user decision; free-text status; next action.
Acceptance Criteria: Columns display existing state and handle missing CV/application data cleanly.
Manual Verification: Inspect rows at different workflow stages.

Title: Workflow-volume KPI cards
Objective: Show simple counts above the Jobs table.
In Scope: Jobs entered; jobs assessed; CVs generated; CVs uploaded; CVs approved.
Acceptance Criteria: Counts use agreed definitions and update after data changes.
Manual Verification: Create sample records and compare manual counts.

Title: Token and time KPI cards
Objective: Show assessment and CV usage totals and averages.
In Scope: Total/average tokens; total/average duration; successful jobs only for averages; separate operations.
Acceptance Criteria: Aggregations are correct and no divide-by-zero errors occur.
Expected Tests: Aggregation tests with mixed success/failure calls.
Manual Verification: Compare KPI values with LLM-call rows.

Title: Failed-task KPI
Objective: Show the number of failed tasks requiring attention and link to Background Runs.
In Scope: Assessment and CV failures; clickable navigation/filter.
Acceptance Criteria: The count matches retryable failed tasks and opens the relevant filtered screen.
Manual Verification: Create a failed dummy task and use the card.

Title: Document B lane-to-section configuration
Objective: Create deterministic configuration mapping each CV lane to required Document B sections.
In Scope: Fixed sections; primary lane; optional secondary lane; evidence-related section references.
Acceptance Criteria: Every supported lane resolves to an explicit ordered section set.
Manual Verification: Inspect mappings for architecture, deployment and Head of Data.

Title: Document B supplementary retrieval
Objective: Add optional semantic retrieval that supplements, but never replaces, deterministic sections.
In Scope: Queries from JD/evidence gaps; top chunks; source metadata; deduplication.
Out of Scope: Using retrieval to choose the primary lane or omit mandatory sections.
Acceptance Criteria: Mandatory sections are always present and supplementary chunks are traceable.
Manual Verification: Review retrieved context for two unlike jobs.

Title: CV context builder
Objective: Assemble the complete CV-generation packet.
In Scope: JD; assessment; selected lane; fixed B rules; routed sections; supplementary passages; versions.
Acceptance Criteria: Context is reproducible, authorised and contains no unrelated lane by default.
Manual Verification: Preview packets for primary-only and primary+secondary lanes.

Title: Generic ordered prompt-pipeline engine
Objective: Run configured prompt stages sequentially with persisted intermediate outputs.
In Scope: Stage order; prior output input; retries; status; model/usage metadata; restart from failed step.
Acceptance Criteria: A dummy multi-stage pipeline completes, fails and restarts from the failed stage correctly.
Expected Tests: Pipeline integration tests with fake handlers.
Manual Verification: Run a three-stage dummy pipeline.

Title: English pipeline stage 1
Objective: Implement the first existing CV-generation prompt exactly as configured.
In Scope: Context injection; output persistence; usage capture.
Out of Scope: Later prompt stages or DOCX generation.
Acceptance Criteria: Stage 1 runs for one job and its full output is stored.
Expected Tests: Mocked model-call tests; opt-in integration test.
Manual Verification: Compare output with the current manual Phase 1.

Title: English pipeline stage 2
Objective: Implement the second configured English CV prompt using stage 1 output.
In Scope: Ordered input; persistence; usage; failure handling.
Acceptance Criteria: Stage 2 receives the approved stage 1 output and stores its result.
Manual Verification: Inspect the request and response for one job.

Title: English pipeline stage 3
Objective: Implement the third configured English CV prompt.
In Scope: Ordered input; persistence; usage; failure handling.
Acceptance Criteria: Stage 3 runs only after stage 2 and can be retried independently.
Manual Verification: Force a failure and retry only stage 3.

Title: English pipeline stage 4
Objective: Implement the final English critique/rewrite prompt.
In Scope: Ordered input; final text; usage; anti-overclaiming output.
Acceptance Criteria: A complete English pipeline produces the final approved-for-rendering content.
Manual Verification: Compare the final output with a manual four-step example.

Title: Final structured-CV schema
Objective: Define the fields required by the Word templates and validate final model output.
In Scope: Headline; profile; skills; employer blocks; bullets; independent work; education; additional information.
Acceptance Criteria: Representative final outputs parse without losing content and required fields are enforced.
Manual Verification: Convert one prior final CV into the schema.

Title: English template placeholder mapping
Objective: Inspect the actual English template and document the structured-field mapping.
In Scope: Paragraphs; tables; placeholders/content controls; repeatable bullet areas; fixed text.
Acceptance Criteria: Every generated field has a target and all fixed content is identified.
Manual Verification: Review the mapping against the Word template.

Title: English DOCX renderer
Objective: Populate a copy of the English template while preserving formatting.
In Scope: Text replacement; bullet insertion; optional sections; local output.
Out of Scope: Editing inside Streamlit or PDF conversion.
Acceptance Criteria: A valid DOCX opens in Word and matches the template layout without unresolved placeholders.
Manual Verification: Open the generated CV and visually inspect both pages.

Title: Generated filename and folder rules
Objective: Save every generated CV in one configured folder using the agreed naming convention.
In Scope: resume - Daniel Brule - YYYY-MM-DD - Company.docx; Windows sanitisation; collision suffix.
Out of Scope: Subfolders by company or keeping Word version history.
Acceptance Criteria: Names are deterministic, valid on Windows and collisions do not overwrite files silently.
Expected Tests: Unit tests for invalid characters and collisions.
Manual Verification: Generate twice for a company containing special characters.

Title: CV database model and generation metadata
Objective: Store the active CV file and its generation state against the job.
In Scope: Source; status; language; path; lane; versions; approval date; errors.
Out of Scope: Multiple active CV versions per job.
Acceptance Criteria: A generated CV record links to an existing file and captures all relevant versions.
Manual Verification: Inspect a generated CV record.

Title: CV generation worker handler
Objective: Connect a queued CV task to the full English pipeline and renderer.
In Scope: Pipeline; structured validation; rendering; task and CV statuses; independent failures.
Acceptance Criteria: One queued job reaches READY_FOR_REVIEW with a DOCX or a clear failed state.
Expected Tests: End-to-end service test with mocked OpenAI.
Manual Verification: Queue one English CV and inspect the file.

Title: Generate selected CVs action
Objective: Queue CV generation for multiple selected eligible jobs in one click.
In Scope: Eligibility; selected status; one task per job; batch ID; confirmation.
Acceptance Criteria: Eligible selected rows are queued and one failure does not block others.
Manual Verification: Generate CVs for several selected jobs.

Title: Regenerate selected CVs
Objective: Allow one or more generated/failed CVs to be relaunched manually.
In Scope: Full restart option; overwrite active generated file only after success; collision handling.
Acceptance Criteria: Prior usable file remains until replacement succeeds and several rows can be relaunched.
Expected Tests: Integration tests for successful and failed regeneration.
Manual Verification: Regenerate two CVs, forcing one failure.

Title: Upload an existing CV
Objective: Associate a user-provided DOCX with a job instead of generating one.
In Scope: DOCX validation; copy to uploaded folder; source UPLOADED; READY_FOR_REVIEW.
Out of Scope: Parsing or rewriting the uploaded CV.
Acceptance Criteria: A valid DOCX is linked to the job and an invalid file is rejected.
Manual Verification: Upload an existing CV and reopen the job.

Title: CV tab in Job Details
Objective: Show the active CV and the correct generate/upload/review actions.
In Scope: Source; status; filename/path; generation errors; upload; regenerate; open; approve.
Out of Scope: In-app Word-like editor or file history.
Acceptance Criteria: Available actions match the job/CV state and no invalid transition is offered.
Manual Verification: Walk through no-CV, generated, uploaded, failed and approved states.

Title: Open CV from Job Details
Objective: Open the associated DOCX using the Windows default application.
In Scope: Path validation; OS launch; useful missing-file error.
Acceptance Criteria: The correct file opens and missing files do not crash the app.
Expected Tests: Unit test for path checks; OS call mocked.
Manual Verification: Click Open CV on a local Windows machine.

Title: Open CV from Jobs dashboard
Objective: Provide a direct Open CV action in the main table.
In Scope: Visible action when a file exists; reuse secure path-opening service.
Acceptance Criteria: The dashboard opens the correct CV without entering Job Details.
Manual Verification: Open generated and uploaded CVs from separate rows.

Title: Approve CV
Objective: Allow the user to mark the Word-reviewed file as approved.
In Scope: Approval button; confirmation; date; status; optional review note.
Out of Scope: Verifying whether the file was actually edited in Word.
Acceptance Criteria: Approval is explicit, persists and is visible on dashboard and details.
Manual Verification: Approve a CV and confirm the KPI/status changes.

Title: French-reference indexing
Objective: Make prior French CVs available as style and terminology references.
In Scope: Versioned files; vector store or searchable collection; source metadata.
Out of Scope: Treating reference CV claims as evidence.
Acceptance Criteria: Relevant passages can be retrieved and are clearly labelled as style references only.
Manual Verification: Search for French profile and education phrasing.

Title: French adaptation context builder
Objective: Prepare the first additional French step after the complete English pipeline.
In Scope: English final output; French prompt 1; French examples; French template conventions; evidence-preservation rules.
Acceptance Criteria: The French context always starts from final English content and preserves metrics and evidence.
Manual Verification: Inspect the context for one French job.

Title: French adaptation stage
Objective: Run the first additional French prompt.
In Scope: Language adaptation; terminology; country/location conventions; output persistence and usage.
Acceptance Criteria: A complete French draft is produced after English stage 4.
Manual Verification: Compare with a literal translation and French references.

Title: French review and rewrite stage
Objective: Run the second additional French prompt to review and rewrite the adaptation.
In Scope: Natural French; anti-overclaiming; consistency; final output persistence.
Acceptance Criteria: The final French output is internally consistent and follows the same evidence as English.
Manual Verification: Review metrics and employment facts against English output.

Title: French structured-output validation
Objective: Validate that final French content fits the common CV schema and preserves factual anchors.
In Scope: Schema parse; number/date/name consistency checks; language-specific labels.
Acceptance Criteria: Changed metrics, dates or employer names are detected before rendering.
Manual Verification: Introduce a deliberate metric change and confirm rejection.

Title: French template mapping and renderer
Objective: Populate the French DOCX template using the validated final French output.
In Scope: Template mapping; fixed French text; common output folder and naming convention.
Acceptance Criteria: The French DOCX opens correctly and contains no English-template remnants.
Manual Verification: Open and visually compare with previous French CVs.

Title: French CV worker integration
Objective: Route FR jobs through four English stages plus the two French stages and French renderer.
In Scope: Language dispatch; task states; usage aggregation across all six stages.
Out of Scope: Skipping English generation for French jobs.
Acceptance Criteria: A queued FR job completes all six stages and reaches READY_FOR_REVIEW.
Manual Verification: Generate one French CV and inspect Background Runs.

Title: Contact database model
Objective: Support multiple contacts per job.
In Scope: Name; title; LinkedIn; interview date; notes; timestamps.
Acceptance Criteria: A job can have several contacts and deleting a job handles contacts safely.
Manual Verification: Add two contacts to one job.

Title: Contact management UI
Objective: Add, edit and delete contacts over time in the Application tab.
In Scope: Contact table; form; validation; LinkedIn link.
Acceptance Criteria: Multiple contacts can be maintained and changes persist.
Manual Verification: Add and edit contacts with different interview dates.

Title: Application fields UI
Objective: Edit the basic application record from Job Details.
In Scope: Free-text status; application date; next action/date; salary; notes; closure reason.
Out of Scope: Fixed company-specific interview-stage enum or work-authorisation field.
Acceptance Criteria: All fields save independently and free-text status accepts company-specific wording.
Manual Verification: Record several different company processes.

Title: Application columns and filters
Objective: Expose basic application tracking on the Jobs dashboard.
In Scope: Status; next action; next-action date; filters and sorting.
Acceptance Criteria: Dashboard values match Job Details and free-text statuses remain filterable.
Manual Verification: Filter jobs by several custom statuses.

Title: Windows launcher
Objective: Start Streamlit and the local worker with one documented command.
In Scope: Batch/PowerShell launcher; environment check; process shutdown guidance.
Out of Scope: Windows installer or background Windows service.
Acceptance Criteria: A clean local environment can start both processes and display useful startup errors.
Manual Verification: Launch, use and stop the application.

Title: User-facing error handling
Objective: Translate technical failures into actionable UI messages.
In Scope: OpenAI; database; missing assets; DOCX; file paths; worker failures.
Acceptance Criteria: Expected failures do not expose stack traces by default and point to recovery actions.
Manual Verification: Trigger representative errors on each screen.

Title: Input and state validation
Objective: Prevent invalid operations and inconsistent workflow states.
In Scope: Required fields; URLs; dates; active assets; file existence; task eligibility; transition rules.
Acceptance Criteria: Invalid actions are blocked before side effects and valid paths remain usable.
Expected Tests: Unit and integration tests for state transitions.
Manual Verification: Attempt each operation in an invalid state.

Title: English end-to-end workflow test
Objective: Cover the complete English path from job entry to CV approval.
In Scope: Add; assess; pursue; select; generate; open-path validation; approve; tracking.
Acceptance Criteria: The automated test proves the main English workflow with mocked OpenAI and temporary files.
Manual Verification: Run the same flow manually with one real job.

Title: French end-to-end workflow test
Objective: Cover the complete French path including both additional prompts.
In Scope: English stages; French stages; reference retrieval; French rendering; approval.
Acceptance Criteria: The automated test proves six-stage routing and factual consistency checks.
Manual Verification: Generate and review one real French CV.

Title: Installation, backup and operating guide
Objective: Document setup, routine operation and recovery for the local application.
In Scope: Python setup; API key; startup; asset replacement; DB/file backup; inactive store cleanup; troubleshooting.
Acceptance Criteria: A user can install and operate the system from the guide without undocumented steps.
Manual Verification: Follow the guide on a clean checkout.

Title: Final privacy and repository audit
Objective: Confirm private career data, generated CVs and secrets cannot be committed accidentally.
In Scope: .gitignore; sample config; log redaction; repository scan; local-data documentation.
Acceptance Criteria: No private files or secrets are tracked and logs exclude full sensitive content by default.
Manual Verification: Run git status after normal use and inspect logs.
